origin.name: Tanzania
origin.key_document: Death certificate from local authority (requires certified English translation if in Swahili)
origin.source: FCDO Tanzania travel advice, updated 2025; British High Commission Dar es Salaam guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Tanzania is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option alongside full body repatriation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Death certificate from local authority (certified English translation required if in Swahili)
special_rules: Deaths on Zanzibar may involve additional logistics for transfer to the mainland before air repatriation.
Swahili-language documentation requires certified English translation for UK acceptance.
Police clearance is required for all violent or suspicious deaths.
An export permit from Tanzanian authorities is required for all repatriations.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British High Commission in Dar es Salaam can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Tanzania to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Tanzania. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-3 weeks. Local registration, export permits, and air cargo all handled. Contact us 24/7.
